title: 40-minute grandma morning care routine — coffee, yogurt, salad, dog food, instructions
html:
40-minute grandma morning care routine — coffee, yogurt, salad, dog food, instructions

Source: 2025-08-05 Apple Note. The caregiving SOP for Grandma Yahir wrote himself — preferences (right-handed silverware, small bite size, fridge door open-order). 16 days before the Austin departure.

TL;DR

  • Title literal: 40 minutes is the duration of the morning routine.
  • Personal preferences captured: right-handed spoons/forks, small bite size, fridge left-then-right (do not slam).
  • Coffee protocol: 2 scoops, leave so she can do it, fill water to top line.
  • Yogurt + strawberry: 1 scoop granola, 1 dollop yogurt, strawberry tops cut, cubed horizontal then vertical, 2 big strawberries, mix the yogurt — give to grandma.
  • Lunch prep (shrink-wrapped for later): salad (2/3 bowl, 1.5" cucumber, sprinkle of carrots and lettuce, 3 baby tomatoes sliced), pre-hard-boiled eggs (8-part cut, half), soup split into 2 bowls.
  • Toast at the 2-min mark.
  • Dog food: 2 ounces.
  • Closing: take out coffee filter, replace water, get dog plate to sink, ask what she wants for dinner and open her food.

Pattern observed

This is Yahir-as-caregiver, captured operationally. He treats Grandma's morning the way he treats Brotein onboarding flows or NaviWell ECW workflow — write down the SOP so it's repeatable and quality is consistent. The note is also a tender record of attention to detail (right-handed silverware, fridge-don't-slam) — care expressed as procedure.

Written 2 weeks before he leaves Oregon for Austin. The act of writing this down is part of the handoff — preserving the routine for whoever takes over (likely emilio or mom).

This is also the third 2025 note about Yahir-as-family-anchor-during-decline — see 2024 10 31 dominic rebuild and 2025 06 21 on bubbas dominic for the cousin/uncle context, and 2025 03 15 life feels heavy for the dad context.

Open questions

  • Is this Grandma Liz (mentioned in 2024 07 04 questions) or grandma on the other side?
  • Was the routine successfully handed off?
  • Did Yahir keep doing this remotely (sending mom reminders) after moving?
